content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
August 28,
1992
Patrick L.
Enright
JACKSON,
HARGROVE,
EMERICH
PEDREIRA
&
NAHIGIAN
7108 North Fresno Street,
Fourth Floor
Fresno,
CA
93720—2938
—
Re:
Your Request for Advice
Our File No.
A—92-431
Dear Mr.
Enright:
This letter is in response to your request for advice
regarding the duties and responsibilities of City of Coalinga
Councilmember James Allen under the conflict—of—interest
provisions of the Political Reform Act
(the “Act”).
1
QUESTION
Do the conflict—of-interest provisions of the Act require
Councilmember Allen to disqualify himself from participating in
city council decisions regarding the Webb House Restoration
Foundation
(the “foundation”)?
CONCLUSION
The Webb House Restoration Foundation is a nonprofit
corporation and is not a source of income to Councilmeinber Allen.
Accordingly,
the conflict—of-interest provisions of the Act do not
require the councilmember to disqualify himself from participating
in city council decisions regarding the foundation.
FACTS
Councilmember Allen is on
the board of directors of the
foundation,
a nonprofit organization.
He does not receive any
- Government Code Sections 81000—91015.
All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission
regulations appear at
2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000,
et q.
All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division
6 of the California Code of Regulations.
428 J Street, Suite 800 •
P.O. Box 807 •
Sacramento, CA
95812 •
(916) 322-5660


Our File No. A-92-431
Page
2
salary or compensation as a member of the board of directors.
The
Webb House is a historical landmark in the city.
The foundation’s
goal is to restore the Webb House,
and has received approximately
$12,000 in donations, with another $15,000 pledged.
The
foundation’s goal is to begin construction once $50,000 has been
raised.
The Webb House is located on city property in the
redevelopment area.
The city council is also the governing board
of the city redevelopment agency.
The city council and/or
redevelopment agency will be considering issues regarding the Webb
House and the attempts to restore it.
In particular, the city may
enter into a contract with the foundation regarding the use of the
city property and potential liabilities during the construction of
the house.
The city or redevelopment agency may desire, at some
future date, to grant funds or loan money to the foundation.
Your firm is the city attorney for the City of Coalinga.
In
this capacity,
you seek our advice to determine whether
Councilmember Allen may participate in decisions regarding the
Webb House and vote on these matters when they come before the
city council.
ANALYSIS
Section 87100 prohibits public officials from making,
participating in,
or using their official position to influence a
governmental decision in which they know or have reason to know
they have a financial interest.
An official has a financial
interest in a decision if it is reasonably foreseeable that the
decision will have a material financial effect, distinguishable
from its effect on the public generally,
on the official,
or on a
member of the official’s immediate family,
2 or on:
(a)
Any business entity in which the public
official has a direct or indirect investment worth
one thousand dollars
($1,000)
or more.
(b)
Any real property in which the public
official has a direct or indirect interest worth
one thousand dollars
($1,000)
or more.
(c)
Any source of income, other than gifts
and other than loans by a commercial lending
institution in the regular course of business on
terms available to the public without regard to
official status, aggregating two hundred fifty
dollars
($250)
or more in value provided to,
received by or promised to the public official
2
An official’s “immediate family” includes his spouse and
dependent children.
(Section 82029.)


Our File No. A-92-431
Page
3
within 12 months prior to the time when the
decision is made.
(d)
Any business entity in which the public
official is a director, officer, partner, trustee,
employee,
or holds any position of management.
(e)
Any donor of,
or any intermediary or
agent for a donor of,
a gift or gifts aggregating
two hundred fifty dollars
($250)
or more in value
provided to, received by,
or promised to the public
official within
12 months prior to the time when
the decision is made.
Section 87103.
Councilmember Allen is a public official.
(Section 82048.)
Therefore,
he may not make, participate in making,
or in any way
us his official positin to attempt to influence a decision in
which he has a financial interest.
(Section 87100.)
A decision to enter into a contract or to grant funds or lend
money to a business entity may create a conflict of interest if a
public official is a director, officer, partner, trustee,
employee
‘1ds a position of management in the business entity.
(Section 87103(d).)
However, the term “business entity” means any
organization operated for profit.
(Section 82005.)
Since the
foundation is a nonprofit organization, it is not a “business
entity” as that term is used in Section 87103(d).
In addition,
you stated that the foundation is not a source of income to
Councilmember Allen.
Accordingly, his participation on the board
of directors of the foundation does not create a conflict of
interest with respect to his official duties as a councilmember.
Thus,
Councilmember Allen may participate in governmental
decisions regarding the foundation.
Please note, however, that our advice to you is limited to
the provisions of the Political Reform Act.
Other provisions of
law,
such as Section 1090 of the Government Code or the general
doctrine of incompatible offices may have some bearing on this
matter.
We suggest that you contact the Office of the Attorney
General for advice as to these matters.


Our File No.
A-92-431
Page
4
We trust this letter adequately responds to your inquiry.
Should you have any further questions regarding this matter,
do
not hesitate to call me at
(916)
322—59O1.
Sincerely,
Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel
By:
Blanca H. Breze
Counsel,
Legal Division
SH:BMB:aa
Copies of Commission regulations and Opinions are available in
many law libraries.
Alternatively, copies of these materials and
Commission advice letters may be obtained from the Commission at a
cost of lO per page.

in city council decisions regarding the foundation.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Do the conflict—of-interest provisions of the Act require
Councilmember Allen to disqualify himself from participating in
city council decisions regarding the Webb House Restoration
Foundation
(the “foundation”)?

CONCLUSION: The Webb House Restoration Foundation is a nonprofit
corporation and is not a source of income to Councilmeinber Allen.
Accordingly,
the conflict—of-interest provisions of the Act do not
require the councilmember to disqualify himself from participating
in city council decisions regarding the foundation.
